trainees.name: Michael Wilson
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** lockups & chain wrestling, criss-cross/drop-down, headlock takeovers (give & take), snap/back bumps, shoulder tackles, back elbows, arm drags, hip tosses, drop-toe-hold (giver), DDT (give & take), enzuigiri, big boots, punches/strikes, drop kicks, front/back/superman rolls, fireman's carry (teaches it), bulldogs (give), suplex (give & demo form), body slam (give), kitchen sink (take), sentons, time warp (took for the first time), four-corner selling, pins (schoolboy & small package — demos), promos, five-minute practice matches (heel).

**Recent flags:** None on his own work. July 21 he demonstrated the schoolboy and small package pins for the group and Jimmy used him to check Jack's post mid-suplex; all his own reps (bulldog, arm drag, suplex, body slams) were clean. July 17, July 7, and June 30 were all clean; across recent sessions he's more often the one teaching or demonstrating than being corrected.

Michael is the de facto senior coach of the gym. 116 sessions in, he draws essentially zero correction on his own work and is regularly used by the trainers to demonstrate and teach — on July 21 he demoed the schoolboy and small package pins and served as the giver Jimmy paused mid-suplex to showcase Jack's posting. He leads the group through the fireman's carry, coaches newer trainees through spot one, and anchors clean practice matches. He calls himself a 'jack of all trades, master of none,' and functions as the program's model for self-driven ringwork.

trainees.name: Hailey Stokely
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** lockups & chain wrestling, criss-cross/drop-down, headlock takeovers, selling, forearms/strikes, DDT (give & take), drop kicks, bulldogs (give & take), suplex (give & take), body slam (give/take, used as demo model), second-rope hurricanrana, second-rope frog splash, super kick, spot one (coaches it), leapfrog (jumper), four-corner selling, pins (crucifix — demo), promos, five-minute practice matches (heel).

**Recent flags:** None recently on her own work. July 21 she demonstrated the crucifix pin for the group and coached the bulldog with Jack (both new to the move); her own bulldog, suplex, and body slam reps were clean. July 17 she demonstrated chain-wrestling examples and ran one-on-one coaching for Dalton on selling and wrist locks; clean 5-minute match vs. Brandon. July 7 and June 30 also clean.

Hailey is a veteran trainee operating at near-trainer level. She draws almost no correction and increasingly does the correcting — on July 21 she demoed the crucifix pin and walked newer trainees through the bulldog, and on July 17 Jimmy brought her up to demo chain-wrestling for the room. She leads and calls her practice matches, works a polished heel, and continues locking in the hurricanrana ahead of the next TAW show. She sets the standard in the room.

trainees.name: Jacob Justice
trainees.notes: Marked inactive May 12, 2026 after six consecutive absences from April 7 onward. Not expected to return to training.
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** selling, rope-throw drills, bumps.

**Recent flags:** Flagged as timid and slow on rope-throw drills (April 7) and as an injury risk for landing elbow-first on bumps. No sessions since to work on either.

Jacob's selling improved visibly through early spring, but confidence and pacing were his biggest gaps and he hasn't attended a session since April 7 — six-plus consecutive absences. Marked inactive May 12; not expected back. History preserved.

trainees.name: Tyler Skelton
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** lockups & chain wrestling, front/superman rolls, headlock takeovers, drop kicks, bulldogs (give & take), arm drags (give & take), suplex (take & give), body slam (give & take), drop-toe-hold (giver), face bumps (take), kick + back forearm, German suplex, striking/corner exercise, five-minute practice matches.

**Recent flags:** July 21 — his suplex to Jeff went wrong on the jump and Jeff landed on his head (safety note on the give); his arm drag to Brandon was near-perfect but needed a touch more speed and to come out from the ropes more. Back to full participation after the June 30 knee issue. June 12 — struggled to get enough jump height on drop kicks, a size/explosiveness edge to keep working.

Tyler is a big-frame trainee still learning how his height and size shape his ringwork — most of his coaching is about explosiveness (drop-kick height) and playing his size in matches. He's solid and believable when he's on: a standout technical five-minute match against Michael in May, clean lockups, and a German suplex that came together cleanly. Fully back in the mix July 21 after the work knee injury, with the night's one real note being a mistimed suplex jump that dropped Jeff on his head — worth watching his suplex give.

trainees.name: Jay Taylor
trainees.notes: Removed from program April 14, 2026. Blackballed from local wrestling scene due to unsafe work, lack of attendance, refusal to accept criticism.

trainees.name: DC
trainees.notes: Removed from the training program May 15, 2026 by unanimous Wrestler's Court vote following the May 12 promo incident (personal insults toward Hailey). Jimmy, Cody, and Kytel determined Wrestler's Court was the most impartial way to handle the decision. DC pled his case in front of the trainees; each trainee voted in the locker room with reasoning. DC's defense involved disturbing allegations — locker-room vote notes kept separately at Joey's call, not in this record. Prior conduct flag: May 12 promo, called Hailey a bitch, crossed other personal lines.
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** lockups, headlock, two-person rope-crossing drill, spot one, promos.

**Recent flags:** Conduct was the decisive issue — the May 12 promo against Hailey crossed hard personal lines (tried to make her go first 'because she's a woman' despite her being the heel, then called her a bitch). Technically, fundamentals still needed polish: stiff lockups, head dropping in the headlock, drifting on the rope-crossing drill, and timing/coordination off on his first spot-one attempt.

DC was removed from the program by unanimous Wrestler's Court vote on May 15, 2026. He met with Jimmy, Cody, and Kytel about the May 12 promo incident, then pled his case to the trainees before the vote. His technique was still developing, but the removal was about conduct, not ringwork — personal insults were made clear as a hard line.

trainees.name: Dalton
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** lockups, chain wrestling, criss-cross/drop-down, strikes/punches, big boots, DDT (give), drop kicks, enzuigiri, headlock takeovers, wrist locks (take), front/back/superman rolls, snap bumps, flip bumps, shoulder tackles, back elbows, hip tosses, arm drags, dropdowns, suplex (take), face bumps, double downs, four-corner selling, refereeing, promos, five-minute practice matches.

**Recent flags:** July 17 — another rough, most-coaching-intensive night: straighten rolls; ran crooked and dropped down too slow on criss-cross; repeated no-selling and bad wrist-lock form in chain wrestling (pulled for extensive one-on-one on how/why to sell from Jimmy, Hailey, Cody); foot placement giving a headlock and body placement taking a wrist lock; his headlock takeover was more a 'roll onto Damian's head' and a later attempt didn't lock up correctly (drill cut short). Reffed for the first time — hands in pockets, told to be louder and mind the workers' positioning. July 7 — rough spot-one night (finger-lock form, foot switching, forgetting the shoulder-tackle taker, rushing, running crooked) and same moves all around on four corners.

Dalton is consistently the most coaching-intensive trainee in the program — he absorbs more correction than anyone, but he absorbs it within the session and keeps grinding. His recurring edges are selling (still the big one), staying square (not running crooked), locking up cleanly, and remembering placements under speed. July 17 added a new dimension: his first turn as a referee, where Jimmy wants him louder and more aware of the workers. When it clicks he strings together genuinely clean work (his enzuigiri came together in June), but selling and headlock-takeover form remain active projects. Steady grinder who keeps taking his reps.

trainees.name: Jack
trainees.notes: Age 14. Non-bumping restriction lifted May 5, 2026 — parent always present at training.
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** chops, bumps & selling, spot one, hip toss, DDT (take & give), strikes, drop kicks, enzuigiri (+ step-up), lockups, criss-cross/drop-down, headlock takeovers (give), headlock-to-wrist-control, kitchen sink (take), front/back/superman rolls, snap bumps, shoulder tackles, back elbows, heat-drill selling, bulldogs (give & take), arm drags, suplex (take & post), body slam (take), flip bumps, drop-toe-hold (give & take), chain wrestling, tag practice matches, promos.

**Recent flags:** July 21 — didn't jump enough on Jeff's first body slam so it couldn't be completed (much better second attempt); still relatively new to the bulldog and arm drag, so he pre-coached and walked through them, and both went well (Jimmy had him repeat the arm drag to lock in the form). Jimmy also had him post mid-suplex to show the group — posted well. July 17 — slight timing correction on the criss-cross; in the tag match, told to slow down, flagged for not laying his moves in and working the wrong arm once.

Jack is 14 and on a clear fast track. His signature pattern is brief coaching followed by clean execution — first-ever DDT, back rolls, headlock takeovers, a kitchen sink, and now clean bulldogs and arm drags all landed on minimal instruction, and Jimmy used his suplex post as a teaching example on July 21. He's a standout on the mic and rides confidence well once a psychological block is cleared. His current edges are the newer-trainee staples — pacing (slow down), laying moves in, jumping enough to take moves like the body slam — plus locking in match details like working the correct arm. Parent always present.

trainees.name: Nick
trainees.notes: Jack's dad. Officially joined the program April 14, 2026. Left the program June 2, 2026 after disclosing pre-existing back and nerve problems — decided he didn't want wrestling to exacerbate them. History preserved.
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** snap bumps, lockups (incl. lockup-to-corner push), headlock, face bumps, drop-toe-hold (give & take), body slam (give & take), five-minute practice match (babyface).

**Recent flags:** May 26 — ghost bumped (bumped too early) on his first snap bump, landed wrong, and hurt his back, sitting out the rest of the night. That early-bump habit had now caused an injury.

Nick — Jack's dad — was one of the easiest trainees in the gym to coach, usually absorbing corrections on the first rep, and had grown from rep work into holding his own in a full five-minute practice match (a clean babyface comeback win over heel Hailey). He left the program June 2, 2026 after disclosing pre-existing back and nerve problems, deciding he didn't want wrestling to worsen them; the May 26 back injury likely factored in. History preserved.

trainees.name: Brandon
trainees.notes: Joined the program May 5, 2026 from Cody McCulley's personal roster. Has been training for a while and has worked a few matches at TAW.
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** front/back rolls, flip bumps, four-corner selling, chain wrestling, criss-cross/drop-down, dropdowns, hip tosses, arm drags (give & take), lockups, DDT (give), drop kicks, enzuigiri, superman rolls, headlock takeovers (give & take), kitchen sink (take), bulldogs (take), face bumps (take), big boots, double downs, snap bumps, shoulder tackles, back elbows, heat drill, spot one, suplex (give & take), body slam (give & take), striking/corner exercise, tag practice matches, promos, five-minute practice matches.

**Recent flags:** July 21 — when taking Michael's bulldog it was pointed out he catches himself by the knees on face bumps instead of going all the way down; Cody coached him on how to land. Otherwise clean across arm drags (both ways), suplexes, and body slams. July 17 — tripped over Dalton and face-planted on the criss-cross/drop-down drill; told to slow down in the tag match; clean 5-minute match vs. Hailey. July 10 — told to diversify his moves on the four-corner drill.

Brandon joined from Cody McCulley's roster with prior TAW match experience, and it shows in how cleanly and quickly he absorbs new material. Thirteen sessions in, most of his work draws no notes. His current technical project from July 21 is his face-bump landing — catching himself by the knees rather than committing to the bump — alongside his standing growth areas of in-ring vocabulary (four-corner variety), promo stamina, and match pacing. Reliable, low-maintenance, and comfortable working full practice matches on both sides.

trainees.name: Jeff
trainees.notes: Joined the program May 5, 2026 from Cody McCulley's personal roster. Relatively new to training.
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** five and fives, front rolls, flip bumps, hip tosses, four-corner selling, chain wrestling, criss-cross/drop-down, arm drags (give & take), DDT (give), Irish whips, drop kicks, headlock takeovers (give & take), bulldogs, big boots, punches, double downs, back rolls, snap bumps, shoulder tackles, back elbows, heat drill, drop-toe-hold (give), lockups, suplex (give & take), body slam (give), striking/corner exercise, spot one, tag practice matches, promos, five-minute practice matches.

**Recent flags:** July 21 — landed on his head when Tyler's suplex jump went wrong (safety incident on the take — no fault noted on Jeff). His own work was clean: arm drags both ways with Damian, a good suplex given to Brandon, and a body slam on Jack (Jack's under-jump, not Jeff's, stalled the first attempt). July 17 — no flags; criticism-free 5-minute match vs. Michael. July 10 — Cody wants a proper flip bump, not the 'fat boy flip bump' form Jeff originally learned. July 7 — one shared spot-one flag with Dalton.

Jeff is relatively new and his current growth edge is bumping mechanics — flip bumps and getting enough jump height (which has held back his suplex). But the trajectory is clearly up: clean own-work on July 21 across arm drags, suplex give, and body slam (with the one scary moment being him landing on his head on Tyler's mistimed suplex jump), a criticism-free match against Michael on July 17, and fast correction pickup. Solid, coachable, and steadily tightening his fundamentals.

trainees.name: Damian
trainees.notes: New trainee, joined June 2, 2026. Name was previously spelled 'Damien' in earlier notes; corrected to 'Damian' June 16, 2026.
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** bumps & selling, spot one, four-corner selling, promos, chain wrestling, criss-cross/drop-down, lockup-to-headlock, headlock takeovers (give & take — first worked July 17), strikes, DDT (give & take), drop kicks, enzuigiri, double downs, big boots, punches, hip tosses, back rolls, lockups, arm drags (give & take), bulldogs, suplex (give & take), body slam (take), striking/corner exercise, tag practice matches, five-minute practice matches.

**Recent flags:** July 21 — funky taking Michael's arm drag (needed more upward/forward momentum), improved on the second attempt and after Cody gave him one to feel it; also jumped into Hailey's suplex before coaching fixed it (next attempt great). Once walked through, his arm-drag give to Michael was great and he volunteered to go twice on arm drags (clean both ways with Jeff). July 17 — worked a headlock takeover for the first time; flagged in the tag match for a shadow bump and hip-toss form. July 7 — four-corner selling a little robotic.

Damian is a promising new trainee, now eight sessions in. His pattern is a rough first rep on new or awkward material — taking the arm drag, jumping into the suplex — that cleans up fast once a trainer walks him through it or lets him feel the move from the other side. His current lessons remain the finer points of taking moves (momentum, jump timing) and selling/match execution (shadow bumps, hip-toss form, staying in character). Named bumps as his strength; eager, volunteering for extra reps, and coming along quickly.

trainees.name: Jacob Renzo
trainees.notes: New trainee, joined June 2, 2026. Distinct from former trainee Jacob Justice (inactive). Removed from the program June 30, 2026 for booking himself in a match at a promotion without express consent from Cody or Jimmy, despite only having two training sessions under his belt.
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** lockup → headlock → shoulder tackle, selling, strikes, enzuigiri, double downs, big boots, punches, back rolls, lockups, promos.

**Recent flags:** June 16 — Jimmy noticed something off on his enzuigiri; Kaleb spotted he was planting his foot, and after the correction his reps were good (just needs a bit more leg height). His actual work drew little criticism — the issue that ended his run was conduct, not ringwork.

Jacob Renzo showed well in his three sessions — great selling with no notes, clean strikes, and quick corrections. He was removed from the program June 30, 2026 after booking himself in a match at an outside promotion without permission from Cody or Jimmy, with only two training sessions in. Jimmy used the removal as a teaching point (Hailey cited the Mass Transit vs. New Jack ECW incident as a cautionary example). Distinct from former trainee Jacob Justice.

trainees.name: Jaden
trainees.notes: New trainee, joined June 16, 2026. Was previously in the program in 2025 before stepping away from wrestling entirely in October 2025; because of the long gap she is counted as a brand-new trainee in the system, with this prior experience noted here.
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** lockup → headlock → shoulder tackle, selling, strikes, DDT (take & give), drop kicks, enzuigiri.

**Recent flags:** June 16 (debut) — needs to make more sound while selling; strikes needed work on form and being more animated; struggled to get enough height/rotation on drop kicks (Jimmy had her drill the jump-and-turn separately before adding the kick back in, which worked).

Jaden is returning to the program after stepping away from wrestling in late 2025, and is counted as a brand-new trainee despite that prior experience. Her one logged session was a solid debut — clean on the lockup drill, and she worked through her drop-kick and DDT issues within the night. Early days; skills and flags will fill in as she logs more sessions.

trainees.name: Jbird
trainees.notes: New trainee, first session June 30, 2026. 'Jbird' is the only name on record so far — full/real name TBD.
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** chain wrestling.

**Recent flags:** June 30 (debut) — coached through chain wrestling on pacing, foot placement, following Kytel's lead, and general form/placement; Brent also worked with him one-on-one. All normal first-timer coaching.

Jbird is brand new — a promising debut on June 30 paired with guest trainer Kytel, called a good first attempt overall, with the expected first-timer coaching load. Did not take part in the practice matches. Full/real name still TBD.
